item.title: TCH Between Norris Arm Turnoffs Blocked Due To Collision: RCMP
item.dc:creator: Brian Callahan
item.description: Well, b'ys, looks like there’s been a bit of a hullabaloo on the highway out by Lewisporte, eh? Some eejit managed to block both sides of the Trans-Canada just after three this afternoon near Norris Arm. So now every Tom, Dick, and Harry's gotta crawl through Norris Arm like their nan drives, 'cause they're rerouting traffic through there with a slower speed limit. What a day to have somewhere to be! Anyway, VOCM says they'll give us the scoop once they get more info. Seems like we’re in it for the long haul, so may as well put the kettle on, right?
item.content:encoded: Well, b'ys, looks like there’s been a bit of a hullabaloo on the highway out by Lewisporte, eh? Some eejit managed to block both sides of the Trans-Canada just after three this afternoon near Norris Arm. So now every Tom, Dick, and Harry's gotta crawl through Norris Arm like their nan drives, 'cause they're rerouting traffic through there with a slower speed limit. What a day to have somewhere to be! Anyway, VOCM says they'll give us the scoop once they get more info. Seems like we’re in it for the long haul, so may as well put the kettle on, right?

item.title: MPs Thompson, Hutchings Got Trudeau’s Back Amid Calls To Resign
item.dc:creator: Brian Callahan
item.description:
Well, b'ys, here's the scoop: there's a bit of a brouhaha goin' on over in Ottawa. Some folks in the Liberal Party might be sneakin' around trying to get rid of Trudeau—ye know, like passin' notes in drama class, tryin' to get him to pack his bags as leader. Trudeau's been gettin' some side-eye ever since they lost a couple of by-elections in Montreal and Toronto, places they'd count on as sure things, right?
Now, don't go thinkin' everyone's ready to kick him out the door. Our very own MPs, Gudie Hutchings and Joanne Thompson, are standin' by their man. Hutchings is all, "I'm with Justin," like he's the captain of the hockey team or somethin'. Joanne's not far behind, sayin' she's proud to be Liberal and backin' the leader, no matter what rumors are flyin'.
Ah, politics, eh? It's like watchin' a capelin roll—everybody floppin' around tryin' to get somewhere. Maybe they'll figure it out before the next election, or maybe it'll be a good ol' Newfie kitchen party of a mess. We'll see, won't we?
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, here's the scoop: there's a bit of a brouhaha goin' on over in Ottawa. Some folks in the Liberal Party might be sneakin' around trying to get rid of Trudeau—ye know, like passin' notes in drama class, tryin' to get him to pack his bags as leader. Trudeau's been gettin' some side-eye ever since they lost a couple of by-elections in Montreal and Toronto, places they'd count on as sure things, right?
Now, don't go thinkin' everyone's ready to kick him out the door. Our very own MPs, Gudie Hutchings and Joanne Thompson, are standin' by their man. Hutchings is all, "I'm with Justin," like he's the captain of the hockey team or somethin'. Joanne's not far behind, sayin' she's proud to be Liberal and backin' the leader, no matter what rumors are flyin'.
Ah, politics, eh? It's like watchin' a capelin roll—everybody floppin' around tryin' to get somewhere. Maybe they'll figure it out before the next election, or maybe it'll be a good ol' Newfie kitchen party of a mess. We'll see, won't we?

item.title: Tories in Gander for Annual General Meeting
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Well, b'ys, the PC party's got their big gathering this weekend over in Gander at a fancy spot, Quality Inn and Suites. Tonight kicks things off, but the real meat and potatoes happens when Tony Wakeham gets up there tomorrow night for his big speech. He's plannin’ to yak on about what the party will be doin’ once the House of Assembly fires up in the fall.
Wakeham’s got a whole list of what they're hopin’ to get sorted, with some new policies in the mix. He's also yammerin' on about putting focus on the folks here in Newfoundland and Labrador. Apparently, it’s time to stop jawing about the next election and start thinkin’ about the next generation. Seems like a smart move, I suppose, but it’s politics, eh? Could be all talk and no action. We'll see if anythin' actually changes or if it’s just a bunch of puffed-up promises.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, the PC party's got their big gathering this weekend over in Gander at a fancy spot, Quality Inn and Suites. Tonight kicks things off, but the real meat and potatoes happens when Tony Wakeham gets up there tomorrow night for his big speech. He's plannin’ to yak on about what the party will be doin’ once the House of Assembly fires up in the fall.
Wakeham’s got a whole list of what they're hopin’ to get sorted, with some new policies in the mix. He's also yammerin' on about putting focus on the folks here in Newfoundland and Labrador. Apparently, it’s time to stop jawing about the next election and start thinkin’ about the next generation. Seems like a smart move, I suppose, but it’s politics, eh? Could be all talk and no action. We'll see if anythin' actually changes or if it’s just a bunch of puffed-up promises.

item.title: No Criminal Conduct by RNC in Corner Brook Man’s Death: SIRT-NL
item.dc:creator: Brian Callahan
item.description:
Oh me nerves! So here's the big hullabaloo from Corner Brook: the province's police watchdog, SIRT-NL, took a gander at what happened to this fella who died in the lockup on Christmas Day last year. Turns out, they've cleared the two RNC officers from any criminal wrongdoing. The poor fella, bless his heart, kicked off his Christmas celebrations with a bender on booze, cocaine, and prescription meds, and it all went south pretty quick. By midnight, he was already tottering around like a fish out of water, and it looked like he might've overdone the snow and happy pills, so the hotel staff called 911.
Now, the paramedics and police came to wrangle him up and carted him off to the hospital. But get this, the lad tears out his IVs and storms out like he owns the place. Police caught up with him outside, still three sheets to the wind, and without a lick of sense in him to get treated, they lock him up for his own good. After a few hours, they found him unresponsive in the cell and rushed him back to the hospital, but it was too late; he was gone.
The official word is that he accidentally died due to rib fractures and blood loss. Not something your average bayman would check for after a few too many at the pub, is it? Anyways, SIRT-NL said there’s no way the cops could've known he was in that kind of state since he legged it from the hospital before any tests could tell them what was what.
But here’s a twist for ya, there's another probe going on for the correctional officers involved. I s'pose we'll be hearing more on that one soon enough. Merry bloody Christmas, hey!
item.content:encoded:
Oh me nerves! So here's the big hullabaloo from Corner Brook: the province's police watchdog, SIRT-NL, took a gander at what happened to this fella who died in the lockup on Christmas Day last year. Turns out, they've cleared the two RNC officers from any criminal wrongdoing. The poor fella, bless his heart, kicked off his Christmas celebrations with a bender on booze, cocaine, and prescription meds, and it all went south pretty quick. By midnight, he was already tottering around like a fish out of water, and it looked like he might've overdone the snow and happy pills, so the hotel staff called 911.
Now, the paramedics and police came to wrangle him up and carted him off to the hospital. But get this, the lad tears out his IVs and storms out like he owns the place. Police caught up with him outside, still three sheets to the wind, and without a lick of sense in him to get treated, they lock him up for his own good. After a few hours, they found him unresponsive in the cell and rushed him back to the hospital, but it was too late; he was gone.
The official word is that he accidentally died due to rib fractures and blood loss. Not something your average bayman would check for after a few too many at the pub, is it? Anyways, SIRT-NL said there’s no way the cops could've known he was in that kind of state since he legged it from the hospital before any tests could tell them what was what.
But here’s a twist for ya, there's another probe going on for the correctional officers involved. I s'pose we'll be hearing more on that one soon enough. Merry bloody Christmas, hey!

item.title: More Confirmed Cases of Syphilis Identified in Labrador-Grenfell Region
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Well, b'ys, looks like there's trouble brewin' up north in the Labrador-Grenfell Health region. They got themselves a syphilis outbreak up there, of all things. Would ya believe it, 19 cases already confirmed! That's more than we had in the whole last decade. Guess folks forgot how to use those fancy condoms, eh?
Dr. Kevin Lam's got his hands full, sayin' it ain't just us—seems to be goin' on all over Canada and even further afield. Apparently, people ain't getting tested or treated as much either. Now, I dunno 'bout you, but it seems like someone oughta remind everyone that syphilis ain't exactly a new problem. We figured out how to deal with this ages ago!
Anyway, they’re on it, with more cases still bein' looked into. I say, let's hope they sort it before it goes passin' on to the wee ones in their mams' bellies. Take care of yourselves out there, lads; you don't want to be remembered for catchin' something that's right out of an old history book.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, looks like there's trouble brewin' up north in the Labrador-Grenfell Health region. They got themselves a syphilis outbreak up there, of all things. Would ya believe it, 19 cases already confirmed! That's more than we had in the whole last decade. Guess folks forgot how to use those fancy condoms, eh?
Dr. Kevin Lam's got his hands full, sayin' it ain't just us—seems to be goin' on all over Canada and even further afield. Apparently, people ain't getting tested or treated as much either. Now, I dunno 'bout you, but it seems like someone oughta remind everyone that syphilis ain't exactly a new problem. We figured out how to deal with this ages ago!
Anyway, they’re on it, with more cases still bein' looked into. I say, let's hope they sort it before it goes passin' on to the wee ones in their mams' bellies. Take care of yourselves out there, lads; you don't want to be remembered for catchin' something that's right out of an old history book.

item.title: Spooky Display on Winnipeg Street Collecting Donations for Local Food Bank
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Well, b'ys, looks like ol' Roger Nicholl over on Winnipeg Street in St. John's is at it again with his Halloween hullabaloo. You know how he loves to go all out, making the place look like somethin' out of a horror movie. But I guess the fright fest is for a good cause this time around. They're doing a "trick or trunk" setup—yeah, you heard me right.
Turns out, if ya swing by with a can of beans or some Kraft Dinner between 2 and 4 on Saturday, they'll throw some candy your way. Sounds like a bargain, eh? There's a trunk there for gatherin' up the goods for Bridges to Hope, so they're acceptin' donations all day, every day.
He's expecting over a thousand ghouls and goblins to pull through, just like last year. I s'pose it's nice seein' all those people comin' out for a look-see, but you'd think someone could give Roger a medal at this point or maybe a Guinness record for the most spooked Newfoundlanders. Who says Halloween's just for the young ones, eh?
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, looks like ol' Roger Nicholl over on Winnipeg Street in St. John's is at it again with his Halloween hullabaloo. You know how he loves to go all out, making the place look like somethin' out of a horror movie. But I guess the fright fest is for a good cause this time around. They're doing a "trick or trunk" setup—yeah, you heard me right.
Turns out, if ya swing by with a can of beans or some Kraft Dinner between 2 and 4 on Saturday, they'll throw some candy your way. Sounds like a bargain, eh? There's a trunk there for gatherin' up the goods for Bridges to Hope, so they're acceptin' donations all day, every day.
He's expecting over a thousand ghouls and goblins to pull through, just like last year. I s'pose it's nice seein' all those people comin' out for a look-see, but you'd think someone could give Roger a medal at this point or maybe a Guinness record for the most spooked Newfoundlanders. Who says Halloween's just for the young ones, eh?

item.title: Police Investigating Shingles Theft from Catalina Lumber Yard
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Well, b'ys, it looks like someone's got a new roof in the works, and they ain't payin' for it, that's for sure. The RCMP are on the trail of some crafty thief or thieves who went and cut a hole right in the fence at Mackey’s Timber Mart over in Catalina. They swiped 46 bundles of shingles, and I'd bet me boots they're roofin' somethin' with it—$2,500 worth of shingles ain't pocket change!
They made off with the Dakota brand ones, real fancy-like, in Rustic Cedar. Now, unless someone's looking to get their house spruced up for free, they're gonna have to face the music sooner or later. I tell ya, some folks just can't wait for a sale to redo their roofs!
If anyone around knows anything about this sneaky operation, they're urged to give a shout to the Bonavista RCMP. In the meantime, might be worth keepin' your eyes peeled for any suspicious roofs poppin' up with shiny new shingles!
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, it looks like someone's got a new roof in the works, and they ain't payin' for it, that's for sure. The RCMP are on the trail of some crafty thief or thieves who went and cut a hole right in the fence at Mackey’s Timber Mart over in Catalina. They swiped 46 bundles of shingles, and I'd bet me boots they're roofin' somethin' with it—$2,500 worth of shingles ain't pocket change!
They made off with the Dakota brand ones, real fancy-like, in Rustic Cedar. Now, unless someone's looking to get their house spruced up for free, they're gonna have to face the music sooner or later. I tell ya, some folks just can't wait for a sale to redo their roofs!
If anyone around knows anything about this sneaky operation, they're urged to give a shout to the Bonavista RCMP. In the meantime, might be worth keepin' your eyes peeled for any suspicious roofs poppin' up with shiny new shingles!

item.title: Torbay Man Charged With Sexual Assault After Incident at Business
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description: Well, b'y, looks like we got ourselves another case of someone bein' a right eejit in Torbay. Eric Tapper, 43, got himself chucked in the clink after the cops were called to some business on Torbay Road. Monday morning, not even time for a cuppa tea, and there's a woman needin' help. The cops reckon a sexual assault was after happenin', so they called in the big guns from the Child Abuse and Sexual Assault Unit. Took 'em a couple days, but they nabbed this fella on Thursday and he's sittin' in custody, waitin' to see the judge. Makes ya wonder what's goin' on in some folks' heads, eh? Can't even go about yer day without runnin' into such foolishness. Anyway, here's hopin' justice does its thing, and folks can keep mindin' their own business without worryin' about this sort of carry-on.
item.content:encoded: Well, b'y, looks like we got ourselves another case of someone bein' a right eejit in Torbay. Eric Tapper, 43, got himself chucked in the clink after the cops were called to some business on Torbay Road. Monday morning, not even time for a cuppa tea, and there's a woman needin' help. The cops reckon a sexual assault was after happenin', so they called in the big guns from the Child Abuse and Sexual Assault Unit. Took 'em a couple days, but they nabbed this fella on Thursday and he's sittin' in custody, waitin' to see the judge. Makes ya wonder what's goin' on in some folks' heads, eh? Can't even go about yer day without runnin' into such foolishness. Anyway, here's hopin' justice does its thing, and folks can keep mindin' their own business without worryin' about this sort of carry-on.

item.title: Handful of St. John’s Fire Trucks Awaiting Inspection
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Oh b'y, here we go again! The City of St. John's still has four fire trucks out there waitin' for some fella with a clipboard to give 'em the green light. Seems like last month, the province gave 'em a slap on the wrist and said, "Get at 'er, you've got two weeks to get these trucks checked at Brookfield Road!”
And wouldn't ya know it, the city says they've got this grand preventative maintenance program. But sure enough, there was a "gap in compliance"—fancy talk for "we dropped the ball." They're hopin' to have all their ducks in a row and those inspection stickers up-to-date by the end of the year. Outta 22 trucks, there were six waitin' for inspections, and now they're down to four. One's gettin' a once-over as we speak.
But at least the folks from the Department of Digital Government and Service NL have closed the original order. So, I guess that's somethin', eh? Hopefully, they’re not waitin’ for da capelin to roll in before gettin' the rest sorted. Talk about slow as molasses in January!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, here we go again! The City of St. John's still has four fire trucks out there waitin' for some fella with a clipboard to give 'em the green light. Seems like last month, the province gave 'em a slap on the wrist and said, "Get at 'er, you've got two weeks to get these trucks checked at Brookfield Road!”
And wouldn't ya know it, the city says they've got this grand preventative maintenance program. But sure enough, there was a "gap in compliance"—fancy talk for "we dropped the ball." They're hopin' to have all their ducks in a row and those inspection stickers up-to-date by the end of the year. Outta 22 trucks, there were six waitin' for inspections, and now they're down to four. One's gettin' a once-over as we speak.
But at least the folks from the Department of Digital Government and Service NL have closed the original order. So, I guess that's somethin', eh? Hopefully, they’re not waitin’ for da capelin to roll in before gettin' the rest sorted. Talk about slow as molasses in January!

item.title: NL Hydro Testing Labrador Island Link Today
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description: Oh b'y, NL Hydro's been busy today fiddlin' with the Labrador Island Link, doing what they call "pole trip tests." Fancy way of saying they’re seeing how quick they can flick the lights off and on, I suppose. Anyways, they warned us there might be some short outages, and true to their word, there were a few flickers this morning. But don't worry, they say they're quick about fixing it up – most folks were back and runnin' in about half an hour. Just enough time to boil the kettle again, eh?
item.content:encoded: Oh b'y, NL Hydro's been busy today fiddlin' with the Labrador Island Link, doing what they call "pole trip tests." Fancy way of saying they’re seeing how quick they can flick the lights off and on, I suppose. Anyways, they warned us there might be some short outages, and true to their word, there were a few flickers this morning. But don't worry, they say they're quick about fixing it up – most folks were back and runnin' in about half an hour. Just enough time to boil the kettle again, eh?
